channel.title: blog.freehosts.space
channel.description: The official blog for freehosts.space — guides, comparisons, tutorials, and community spotlights about free hosting.

item.title: How to Deploy a Next.js App for Free in 2026
item.description: A complete walkthrough of deploying your Next.js application to free hosting platforms. From static exports to serverless functions, we cover every option available in 2026.
item.author: mia-santos, joe-clark

item.title: Cloudflare Pages vs Vercel: The Free Tier Showdown
item.description: We put the two most popular free hosting platforms head to head. Bandwidth limits, build times, developer experience, and hidden gotchas — everything you need to choose.
item.author: joe-clark

item.title: Getting Started with Static Site Generators in 2026
item.description: New to static site generators? This beginner-friendly guide explains what they are, why they matter, and how to choose the right one for your project.
item.author: mia-santos

item.title: The State of Free Hosting in 2026
item.description: Our annual roundup of the free hosting landscape. New players, improved tiers, and the platforms that quietly disappeared — here's everything that changed.
item.author: joe-clark, alex-rivera

item.title: Building a Portfolio Site That Actually Gets You Hired
item.description: Your portfolio is your first impression. Here's how to build one that stands out from the template crowd and actually lands you interviews.
item.author: alex-rivera

item.title: Community Spotlight: Open Source Projects Hosted for Free
item.description: Meet five open-source projects that run entirely on free hosting tiers. Their maintainers share what works, what breaks, and what they wish they knew earlier.
item.author: alex-rivera

item.title: Why Every Developer Should Own a Domain Name
item.description: A domain name costs less than a coffee per month. It's the single best investment you can make in your developer identity. Here's why.
item.author: mia-santos

item.title: Mastering Git for Solo Developers
item.description: Git isn't just for teams. A solid Git workflow makes solo development faster, safer, and more organized. Here's the workflow we recommend.
item.author: joe-clark
